intro_script: Welcome back! It's July 7th, 2026. Today's digest covers big leaps in AI trucking transfers, Apollo robot training at Robot Park, and exciting new models for open robotics from NVIDIA and Hugging Face. Let’s dive into the news.
outro_script: That’s your dose of robot news for July 7th! From AI swapping brains like a seamless handshake, keep those circuits humming. Thanks for tuning in; power down smoothly until tomorrow!

items.title: Waabi says its AI driver transferred to Volvo autonomous truck without retraining
items.summary: Waabi announced a significant advancement in autonomous driving by porting its AI-powered virtual driver between different autonomous truck platforms. This capability required no additional retraining, engineering modifications, or new datasets. The company successfully integrated the Waabi Driver software onto the Volvo VNL Autonomous truck model. This demonstrates a key operational leap for their autonomous technology platform.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Waabi says its AI driver transferred to Volvo autonomous truck without retraining." The report details a notable advancement in autonomous driving technology demonstrating the portability of an artificial intelligence-powered virtual driver across disparate vehicle platforms with zero retraining required. Waabi successfully achieved this integration by porting the proprietary Waabi Driver software onto the existing operational framework of the Volvo VNL Autonomous truck model. This process eliminated the need for additional engineering modifications or the incorporation of new, specialized datasets to facilitate the transfer. Such an accomplishment represents a substantial operational milestone for the autonomous technology platform itself. The successful demonstration points toward a significant reduction in deployment friction across various commercial vehicle manufacturers moving forward.

items.title: Apptronik launches Robot Park to train Apollo humanoid robots with Google DeepMind
items.summary: Apptronik has opened an expanded Robot Park in Austin, Texas. This facility serves as the primary data collection and training ground for their humanoid robots. The park is part of a growing global network of such facilities at partner sites worldwide. Apptronik plans to continue expanding this international infrastructure. These resources are dedicated to training advanced AI-powered humanoid robots.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Apptronik launches Robot Park to train Apollo humanoid robots with Google DeepMind." This report details the establishment and function of a new training facility supporting advanced humanoid robotics development. Apptronik has inaugurated an expanded Robot Park located in Austin, Texas. This site operates as the core hub for data collection and specialized training for the company's humanoid units. The park represents one installment in a planned global network of similar infrastructure deployed at various partner locations internationally. These dedicated resources support the advanced AI-powered training processes required by the robots. Continued expansion of this international technical infrastructure is slated to proceed according to Apptronik’s development timeline, signaling further integration of robotics into real-world operational testing environments across multiple geographical regions.

items.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Bring New Models and Frameworks to LeRobot for the Open Robotics Community
items.summary: NVIDIA and Hugging Face are advancing open-source AI and robotics by contributing new models and frameworks to LeRobot for the Open Robotics Community. This initiative aims to accelerate physical AI development within the field. By sharing resources, they address fragmentation that often gates advancements in robotics. Such collaboration provides essential tools, including datasets and foundation models, previously hindered by cost or scarcity. Ultimately, this boosts innovation across open-source robotics efforts.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Blog, "NVIDIA and Hugging Face Bring New Models and Frameworks to LeRobot for the Open Robotics Community." The initiative details a collaboration designed to advance open-source artificial intelligence and robotics through contributions of new models and frameworks to the LeRobot platform. This resource sharing aims to accelerate the development of physical AI within the broader robotics community by addressing historical fragmentation in the field's toolset. Specific contributions include essential datasets and foundation models, which previously faced limitations related to cost or availability. Such shared resources provide crucial tools that directly bolster innovation across various open-source robotics efforts moving forward.

items.title: UK startup Humanoid launches reinforcement learning system to improve robot manipulation
items.summary: Humanoid, a UK robotics and AI firm, launched KinetIQ Ascend, a new reinforcement learning approach for robot manipulation. This system aims to achieve 99.9 percent reliability at human speeds or faster. Building upon the existing KinetIQ platform, it utilizes trial-and-error learning mechanisms. The technology allows Humanoid's robots to improve their performance directly through practicing industrial tasks. Testing of this new system was conducted on various scenarios.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "UK startup Humanoid launches reinforcement learning system to improve robot manipulation." This report details the introduction of a new method designed to elevate the dexterity and reliability of robotic movement in industrial settings. The firm has released KinetIQ Ascend, a novel reinforcement learning approach built upon its established KinetIQ platform. This technology functions by implementing trial-and-error learning mechanisms, allowing the robots to directly refine their capabilities through repeated practice with complex industrial tasks. Initial testing across multiple scenarios demonstrated the system’s potential for achieving operation at human speeds or exceeding them in reliability up to ninety-nine point nine percent. The deployment of such advanced self-improving manipulation systems marks a significant development trajectory for automated physical labor within the technology sector.

items.title: Sonair unveils ‘world’s first’ safety-certified 3D ultrasonic sensor for human-robot collaboration
items.summary: Sonair has introduced the world's first safety-certified 3D ultrasonic sensor designed for human-robot collaboration. Current safety measures, relying on traditional 2D laser scanners, are limited because they cannot detect obstacles above or below a single plane. This limitation represents a major bottleneck in achieving safe coexistence between humans and robots. The new sensor aims to address these shortcomings by providing comprehensive spatial awareness beyond simple perimeters.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Sonair unveils ‘world’s first’ safety-certified 3D ultrasonic sensor for human-robot collaboration." The announcement details the introduction of a novel sensing technology aimed at enhancing the safety parameters for collaborative environments involving humans and robots. Existing industrial safety protocols often depend on traditional two-dimensional laser scanners, which present limitations regarding obstacle detection above or below a single defined plane. This new ultrasonic sensor addresses this by providing comprehensive spatial awareness across a wider volume rather than merely outlining perimeters. The development marks a significant advancement in ensuring reliable coexistence between human personnel and robotic machinery on factory floors and in advanced operational settings. Industry analysts indicate this technology establishes a new benchmark for immersive safety systems within the field of industrial automation.

items.title: Quantum Systems raises $1.2B to expand drone production and deployments
items.summary: Quantum Systems secured \$1.2 billion in funding. This capital infusion is designated for expanding the company's drone production capabilities. The funds will also be used to bolster supply chain resilience across its operations. These investments aim to facilitate broader deployments of their autonomous drone technology. The announcement was originally published on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script:
From The Robot Report, "Quantum Systems raises $1.2B to expand drone production and deployments." This report details a significant financial infusion into the autonomous aerial vehicle manufacturer, Quantum Systems. The company secured one point two billion dollars in funding through recent transactions. These newly acquired funds are earmarked specifically for enhancing the scale of the firm's drone manufacturing operations. Furthermore, the capital will be deployed to strengthen supply chain resilience across all operational sectors of the enterprise.

The investment strategy centers on facilitating the broader and more widespread deployment of Quantum Systems' advanced autonomous drone technology into various markets. Industry analysts note that this substantial backing signals increased confidence in sophisticated unmanned aerial systems infrastructure. The sector is expected to see accelerated integration of such hardware capabilities based on these corporate developments.

items.title: Ambi Robotics and Pickle Robot integrate AI-powered robots to automate inbound warehouse logistics
items.summary: Ambi Robotics and Pickle Robot Company have partnered to automate inbound warehouse logistics. Their integrated system provides an end-to-end solution covering trailer unloading through pallet stacking. This automation addresses a highly labor-intensive segment of warehouse operations. The combined technology was developed specifically for major Fortune 500 retailers and logistics providers. It links the advanced capabilities of both robotics systems together.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Ambi Robotics and Pickle Robot integrate AI-powered robots to automate inbound warehouse logistics." The report details a new automated system designed to manage the entire flow of goods entering a warehouse facility. This integration provides an end-to-end solution that covers multiple stages of receiving operations, from the initial unloading of trailers through to the final stacking of pallets within the facility structure.

The combined technology was engineered specifically for deployment within major Fortune 500 retailers and prominent logistics service providers. By linking the distinct advanced capabilities of both robotic systems together, the partnership addresses a segment of warehouse operation recognized as being highly labor-intensive. This advancement represents a significant step toward streamlining complex supply chain bottlenecks through integrated AI solutions.

This development signals an increased industry focus on comprehensive automation suites for physical goods handling across large commercial logistics centers.

items.title: Ency Software and Stäubli Robotics sign global agreement to ‘simplify robot programming’
items.summary: Ency Software and Stäubli Robotics formed a global partnership to simplify the process of robot programming. This collaboration integrates Stäubli's high-performance industrial robots with Ency Robot, an offline programming solution from Ency Software. The technology suite is designed to improve the ease, speed, and accessibility of robotics programming for advanced automation applications. Specifically, it addresses the challenge of translating complex requirements into workable robotic control. Together, they aim to streamline the entire workflow associated with deploying robots in industrial settings.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Ency Software and Stäubli Robotics sign global agreement to ‘simplify robot programming’." This announcement details a global partnership designed to streamline the process of industrial robot programming for advanced automation applications. The collaboration integrates Stäubli's existing lineup of high-performance industrial robots with Ency Robot, which functions as an offline programming solution provided by Ency Software. This new technology suite is engineered to enhance the ease, speed, and general accessibility of robotic programming across complex operational requirements. By combining these systems, the partnership aims to significantly streamline the entire workflow involved in deploying sophisticated robotics within varied industrial environments. The industry can anticipate improved efficiency metrics across automation integration processes following this technological alignment.

items.title: Kraken Robotics acquires Covelya Group for $615M
items.summary: Kraken Robotics has acquired the Covelya Group for \$615 million. This acquisition is expected to expand Kraken Robotics' product line significantly. Furthermore, it will broaden the company's total addressable market within subsea technology. Information regarding this purchase was initially reported by The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Kraken Robotics acquires Covelya Group for $615M." This story concerns a major corporate acquisition that is set to reshape the subsea technology sector. Kraken Robotics has finalized the purchase of the Covelya Group in a transaction valued at six hundred fifteen million dollars. This move is anticipated to substantially expand the existing product offerings of Kraken Robotics and simultaneously broaden its overall addressable market within specialized underwater technological fields, according to initial reporting on the deal's completion. Industry observers suggest this consolidation signals increased investment focus in deep-sea robotics development for the coming fiscal quarters.

items.title: Robbyant launches LingBot-Depth 2.0 and LingBot-Vision for advanced robotic spatial perception
items.summary: Robbyant has launched two new models: LingBot-Depth 2.0 and LingBot-Vision. LingBot-Depth 2.0 is a next-generation model focused on spatial perception. The release signifies a major advancement in how robots understand their physical environment. These tools will enhance a robot's ability to perceive and navigate the real world with greater accuracy. This development builds upon existing capabilities within robotic AI.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Robbyant launches LingBot-Depth 2.0 and LingBot-Vision for advanced robotic spatial perception." This report details the introduction of two new models designed to significantly advance how robots interpret their surroundings. The launch centers on improving real-world environmental understanding through advanced AI tooling.

The newly released tools include LingBot-Depth 2.0, which represents a next-generation model specifically engineered for enhanced spatial perception tasks. Concurrently, LingBot-Vision was also announced as part of this expansion. These developments mark a major leap in robotic capability, enhancing the platform's capacity to navigate and perceive physical spaces with increased fidelity. This advancement incorporates and builds upon pre-existing sophisticated capabilities within the field of robotic artificial intelligence.

The availability of these models establishes new benchmarks for machine understanding of complex physical environments across various industrial applications.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Adaptation needs in robotic systems
items.summary: Researchers are conducting a study regarding adaptation requirements within robotic systems that utilize Behavior Trees (BTs). Developers working with ROS/ROS 2, autonomous systems, or robot decision-making are being solicited for input. The goal is to assess the relevance of various adaptation needs in robotics. Additionally, the research aims to evaluate how suitable different BT adaptation approaches are for addressing these identified needs. Participation involves completing a 20-minute survey that offers participants an overview of current limitations and ongoing research efforts.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Adaptation needs in robotic systems." Researchers are currently conducting a study focusing on identifying and assessing necessary adaptation requirements within robotic systems that employ Behavior Trees. The research initiative is soliciting detailed input from developers working with frameworks like ROS or ROS 2, as well as those involved in autonomous systems development and robot decision-making processes. Participation requires completing a twenty-minute survey designed to provide an overview of current systemic limitations and ongoing scholarly investigations into the field. The data gathered will help evaluate the suitability of various Behavior Tree adaptation approaches for addressing these identified needs across the industry. This research effort suggests continued focus on enhancing system flexibility within complex robotic deployments.

items.title: Oak Ridge National Lab reveals ‘hidden workforce’ behind AI-powered research facilities
items.summary: Oak Ridge National Laboratory utilizes a hidden workforce to power its AI-driven research facilities. Facilities and Operations (F&O) workers are responsible for building and maintaining the necessary infrastructure. These autonomous laboratories operate with minimal direct human intervention. Instead, their functionality relies on sophisticated robotics, sensors, and automation systems. This unseen team enables advanced scientific research capabilities at the lab.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Oak Ridge National Lab reveals ‘hidden workforce’ behind AI-powered research facilities." The report details how Oak Ridge National Laboratory relies on an unseen group of personnel to operate its sophisticated, artificial intelligence driven research infrastructure. Facilities and Operations workers are responsible for the physical construction and upkeep of these highly automated laboratories. These facilities function with limited direct human involvement, instead depending on advanced robotics, complex sensor arrays, and various automation systems to maintain their operational capacity. This unseen technical team is enabling capabilities in high-level scientific investigation within the facility. The development suggests a growing industry trend toward increased autonomy in specialized research environments requiring substantial physical infrastructure support.

items.title: Boston Dynamics brings its legged robots to the FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Hyundai and Boston Dynamics are preparing their advanced legged robots for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Specifically, the Atlas humanoid robot has been taught footwork through collaboration with Hyundai. Additionally, the Spot robot will be deployed for patrol duties at the event. This announcement originated on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Boston Dynamics brings its legged robots to the FIFA World Cup." This report details preparations by Boston Dynamics and Hyundai to deploy advanced robotic units at the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup event. Specific programming efforts have seen the Atlas humanoid robot undergo footwork training developed through direct collaboration with Hyundai personnel. Furthermore, the Spot model robot is slated for implementation in routine patrol assignments across the tournament grounds. These developments indicate a tangible integration of advanced robotics into major international sporting infrastructure. The continued application of bipedal and quadrupedal AI systems suggests an increasing industry trend toward automating specialized roles within large-scale public venues.

items.title: Robot.com to showcase autonomous advertising robots as official robotics partner of LEAP East
items.summary: Robot.com, a company implementing real-world autonomous robotics, has become the official robotics partner for LEAP East. The company will feature its fleet of autonomous robots at the event's show floor in Hong Kong. Robot.com plans to deploy R-kiwis before an audience highly interested in technology. These robots are designed to showcase various advertising applications (R-ads).
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Robot.com to showcase autonomous advertising robots as official robotics partner of LEAP East." The report details Robot.com's involvement at the upcoming technology exhibition in Hong Kong. The company, which implements real-world autonomous robotics solutions, has been named the official robotics partner for the event. At the show floor, Robot.com intends to feature its collection of autonomous robots, specifically planning the deployment of R-kiwis before an audience segment with a strong interest in technological advancements. These units are designed to demonstrate various applications within the realm of advertising technology, known as R-ads. This partnership signals increased integration of automated hardware into major international tech showcases, setting a benchmark for visible robotics implementation across industry platforms.

items.title: University of Rhode Island opens advanced Ocean Robotics Laboratory for autonomous marine research
items.summary: University of Rhode Island celebrated a significant milestone with the opening of its advanced Ocean Robotics Laboratory. This facility is part of a larger, $300 million revitalization effort for the Narragansett Bay Campus. An underwater ribbon-cutting ceremony was held on June 25th to mark this achievement. During the event, students Elliot Roman and Jake Bonney operated URI's remotely operated vehicle named Rhody. The new lab will advance autonomous marine research efforts at the university.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "University of Rhode Island opens advanced Ocean Robotics Laboratory for autonomous marine research." The story reports on the opening of a new specialized facility designed to bolster underwater technological studies. This laboratory represents a key component of a larger revitalization project at the Narragansett Bay Campus, valued at three hundred million dollars. The official inauguration took place with an underwater ribbon-cutting ceremony held on June twenty-fifth, during which university students successfully operated the remotely operated vehicle named Rhody. The new facility is expected to significantly advance the university’s capacity for autonomous marine research efforts. This development marks a notable expansion in accessible resources for deep-sea robotic investigation and maritime technology integration within academia.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Update on BAGEL (BAG ExpLoration): What's New
items.summary: BAGEL is evolving from a simple viewer into a comprehensive, browser-based ROS debugging tool requiring no native dependencies or ROS installation. Recent updates include client-side bag editing across formats and integrating URDF models for animating robots in the 3D scene with richer mesh rendering. Functionality additions encompass a Bag Health dashboard to analyze topic consistency and exporting panels as videos/images. Notably, version 1.5 introduced live robot data streaming via ws:// URLs directly into an indexed MCAP within the browser. Future plans include adding a 3D measurement tool and improved path visualization for continued development.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Update on BAGEL (BAG ExpLoration): What's New." The update details the evolution of BAGEL into a comprehensive, browser-based tool designed for debugging in the Robot Operating System environment. Recent enhancements allow for client-side bag editing across various formats and integrate URDF models to enable richer mesh rendering and animation of robots within the three-dimensional scene. Functionality additions include a Bag Health dashboard for analyzing topic consistency and new capabilities for exporting panels as videos or images. Notably, version 1.5 introduced live robot data streaming via ws URLs directly into an indexed MCAP format accessible through the browser. Future development efforts plan to incorporate a dedicated 3D measurement tool and improvements to path visualization.

items.title: Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution
items.source: MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech
items.summary: Dairy farmer Anthony Agueda demonstrates the presence of earthworms on his family's land in Hickman, California. The text suggests that worms and microbes are gaining traction as potential solutions for manure pollution. No specific details about how they solve the pollution problem are provided beyond their mention. The passage visually introduces these organisms in an agricultural setting with wood chips and muck.
items.audio_script: From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution." The article discusses the increasing interest in utilizing earthworms and microorganisms as potential remedies for challenges associated with manure pollution. A demonstration was observed at a family farm located in Hickman, California, where earthworms were visible working within an agricultural setting mixed with wood chips and muck. These biological agents are gaining attention for their role in natural remediation processes concerning waste management. This growing application suggests a shift toward bio-based solutions for environmental cleanup challenges in the coming technology sectors.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Last Day for ROSCon Global Early Bird Tickets is Sunday, July 12th
items.summary: Early bird tickets for ROSCon Global 2026 expire on Sunday, July 12th, at 7:00 AM UTC. These tickets offer a discount of $150 off the standard ticket price. Purchasing an early bird pass makes attending the workshop effectively free. Each participant is limited to one post. The deadline for securing this discounted rate is imminent.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Last Day for ROSCon Global Early Bird Tickets is Sunday, July 12th." The report details the closing window for discounted registration to the major robotics conference of 2026. Early bird tickets expire on Sunday, July 12th, at seven o'clock AM Coordinated Universal Time. These passes provide a notable discount equivalent to one hundred and fifty dollars off the standard admission cost, which reportedly makes workshop attendance essentially free for registered participants. The registration window strictly limits each participant to securing only one such pass. This deadline represents an immediate action point for interested parties tracking industry events.

items.summary: RoboBusiness 2026 is currently soliciting expert speakers. This premier event targets developers of commercial robotics and individuals building related businesses. Participation requires submitting proposals through the ongoing call for speakers. Interested parties should note that the submission deadline is approaching quickly. Information regarding this opportunity was first published on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "RoboBusiness 2026 call for speakers closes soon." This report details ongoing speaker solicitations for the upcoming premier event targeting developers of commercial robotics and related enterprises. The process involves interested parties submitting formal proposals through the currently active call for speakers mechanism. Organizers have noted that the submission deadline is approaching rapidly, as previously announced in coverage from The Robot Report. Participation remains contingent upon following the established proposal submission guidelines before the final date. This development suggests a nearing consolidation of industry expertise regarding commercial robotics applications at the scheduled conference.

items.title: GigaWorld-1: A Roadmap to Build World Models for Robot Policy Evaluation
items.summary: Evaluating embodied robot policies is difficult due to slow, costly real-world rollouts, prompting interest in using world models as evaluators. This work introduces WMBench, a benchmark built from real-robot teleoperation data to allow controlled comparisons across various model and encoding types. Analysis of 7 video world models using WMBench showed evaluator quality relies heavily on long-horizon, action-faithful consistency rather than short-term visuals. Findings also indicated that pretraining benefits require balancing general knowledge with robot-specific controllability and that architectural choices significantly impact real-world alignment. Consequently, the authors developed and released \textit{GigaWorld-1}, a world model optimized specifically for policy evaluation in this domain.

items.title: EVA-Client: A Unified Data Collection, Inference, and Deployment Framework for Embodied Policies on Real Robots
items.summary: EVA-Client is an open-source framework designed for deploying, collecting data from, and evaluating manipulation policies on actual robots. It unifies the policy iteration loop stages by sitting between the policy server and the physical hardware. The framework features a component-decoupled architecture, allowing modular additions of different robot backends or inference strategies. EVA-Client supports inspectable execution through Debug, Collect, and Eval workflows, ranging from simulation to real-time control. Furthermore, every evaluation automatically records data in a training-ready format, ensuring continuous feedback for subsequent policy refinement.

items.title: MorphQuad: Morphable Quadrotor for Superhuman Maneuverability, Manipulation, and Resiliency
items.summary: MorphQuad is presented as a morphable quadrotor designed to achieve extreme maneuverability, manipulation, and resiliency for tasks like infrastructure maintenance. Achieving these capabilities requires vectoring maximum thrust in any direction, global stability control, and compact integration onto existing platforms. The system employs a hardware/control co-design where each of the four rotors uses independently articulated two-axis gimbals. Control mechanisms include globally-stable control and energy-optimal thrust allocation to manage inter-rotor interference. Testing confirms MorphQuad's ability to perform multi-revolution rotation, valve manipulation, and robust wind rejection from any source.

items.title: Metallic Ultrasound Waveguides as a Distributed Tactile Sensing Platform for Contact Localization, Force Estimation, and Material Class Discrimination
items.summary: Metallic ultrasound waveguides show promise for advanced tactile sensing by interrogating contacts from one proximal transducer. Experiments using cylindrical indenters demonstrated that applied force during single point indentation is linearly correlated with the reflection-to-transmission coefficient ratio ($F = a \cdot R/T$). Furthermore, the reflected energy partition proved to be a load-independent measure, allowing material classification regardless of contact force. The methodology successfully recovered two separate contact forces from the waveguide signal using two indenters. These findings validate metallic waveguides as a robust platform for simultaneously localizing contacts, estimating forces, and discriminating materials.

items.title: TACO: TActile World Model as a Self-COrrector forScalable VLA Post-Training
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models struggle with contact-rich tasks due to unrecoverable failures undetectable by vision alone. Introducing TACO, a framework, addresses this limitation through tactile-aware world model-driven post-training. TACO operates via a Recognize-Imagine-Label loop using real robot rollouts for iterative improvement. It employs a progress-action model to identify failure states and a visuo-tactile generation model to imagine local corrective segments. Experiments demonstrate that TACO significantly improves success rates on real-world contact manipulation tasks by converting failures into imagined corrections.

items.title: CLEAR: Closed-Loop Reinforcement Learning at Scale for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
items.summary: End-to-end autonomous driving (E2E-AD) using Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models faces performance gaps when moving from imitation learning to closed-loop planning due to distribution shift. CLEAR introduces a system that addresses this by enabling large-scale, closed-loop training through Reinforcement Learning for E2E-AD. The approach learns a novel residual waypoint policy that builds upon knowledge embedded in pretrained VLA policies. To handle the data hunger of vision-based RL, it utilizes a heterogeneous pipeline to maximize parallel simulation environments without resource conflicts. Evaluation demonstrates that CLEAR significantly outperforms existing methods on benchmarks like CARLA longest6 v2 and Bench2Drive using simple reward signals.

items.title: Differential Amplifier-Inspired AmpAttention for Multi-View Robotic Manipulation
items.summary: Proposes AmpAttention, a novel mechanism drawing inspiration from differential amplifiers to mitigate attention drift in multi-view robotic manipulation. This approach aims to suppress noise and capture higher signal-to-noise ratio signals for robust perception. An integrated model called RVAF utilizes task-guided intra-view and inter-view AmpAttention across 18 RLBench tasks. Compared to existing methods, RVAF achieves optimal average success rates while significantly reducing training time by 33.3%. Further enhancing the system with SAM2's image encoder results in RVAF++, which demonstrated a 91% success rate on precise peg insertion tasks.

items.title: DREAMSTEER: Latent World Models Can Steer VLA Policies During Deployment Without Any Finetuning
items.summary: Pretrained Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies struggle with robustness when facing distribution shift during deployment. DREAMSTEER introduces a novel, finetuning-free framework to guide these policies in real-world scenarios. The method utilizes an action-conditioned latent world model to simulate potential outcomes from candidate actions and motion primitives. A language-conditioned value model is then employed to rank the imagined trajectories based on their predicted quality. Testing on four manipulation benchmarks with unseen objects showed DREAMSTEER significantly boosted task success rates (from 23.75% to 66.25%) compared to the base VLA policy.

items.title: Continuous-Time Gaussian Belief Trees for Motion Planning
items.summary: Presents a sampling-based motion planning approach for continuous-time stochastic systems facing process and measurement uncertainties while providing safety guarantees. It models robot dynamics using continuous-time linear stochastic differential equations, with measurements sampled discretely. An offline hybrid belief propagation model tracks belief evolution via continuous ODEs punctuated by Kalman filter updates. Safety is ensured through a novel belief-barrier-function checker that verifies safety over entire continuous trajectory segments. Integration with RRT and SST planners demonstrates high success rates and robust chance constraint enforcement, particularly in narrow passages where discrete methods fail.

items.title: Longitudinal-Motion-Aware Lateral Control for Autonomous Vehicles: A Robust Nonlinear Control Framework
items.summary: Presents a longitudinal-motion-aware robust nonlinear lateral control framework for autonomous vehicles operating in dynamic traffic. The framework develops a tracking error model incorporating varying longitudinal speed and acceleration, using feedback linearization to embed this motion into the control law. Two robust designs are proposed to handle parameter uncertainty: a Lyapunov redesign (LR) inspired by sliding mode control, and an incremental nonlinear dynamic inversion (INDI) method. Both methods are analyzed for stability and proven to ensure ultimate boundedness. Simulations and real-vehicle tests confirm enhanced tracking accuracy across diverse speeds, accelerations, and model uncertainties.
